title: How to visualize *any* Hugging Face model
gist: Embedl's hfviewer.com tool lets users visualize the internal architecture of any Hugging Face deep learning model via URL swap, Chrome extension, or a searchable library of 2,300+ pre-rendered graphs, positioning itself as a missing piece of the HF ecosystem.

insights.claim: hfviewer.com offers three entry points to visualize a Hugging Face model: swapping 'huggingface.co' for 'hfviewer.com' in the URL, a Chrome extension that auto-injects graphs on model pages, and a search bar over pre-rendered graphs.
insights.speaker: Hannes von Essen
insights.so_what: Low-friction distribution (URL swap plus browser extension riding on existing HF traffic) is a growth lever worth watching for adjacent dev-tool startups.
insights.quote: URL swap: when you are on a model at Hugging Face, simply replace "huggingface.co" with "hfviewer.com" to get to the model graph in HF Viewer.
insights.locator: HF Viewer supports three different workflows

insights.claim: The tool ships with a library of over 2,300 instantly viewable model graphs searchable through its search bar.
insights.speaker: Hannes von Essen
insights.so_what: A pre-built corpus of 2,300+ graphs signals meaningful coverage of the popular-model long tail, a moat against trivial clones.
insights.quote: Search for model visualizations, filtering through 2,300+ instantly viewable graphs.

insights.claim: The viewer exposes a granularity slider to move from high-level block structure down to attention internals, and surfaces per-node metrics like FLOPs and channels plus per-edge tensor shapes in an 'edge mode'.
insights.speaker: Hannes von Essen
insights.so_what: Exposing FLOPs and tensor shapes targets advanced users hunting for model-optimization ideas, a more monetizable audience than pure learners.
insights.quote: you can use the granularity slider to explore multiple levels of detail, from the high level block structure of the model to the attention internals

insights.claim: The author frames the tool as serving two distinct audiences at once: advanced users seeking optimization ideas and ML beginners using it as a learning tool to look up unfamiliar concepts in popular models.
insights.speaker: Hannes von Essen
insights.so_what: A dual-audience pitch can broaden TAM but risks diluted product focus; watch which segment the roadmap actually prioritizes.
insights.locator: This will help you understand the architecture

insights.claim: Logged-in users can create visualizations and graph cards for their own models and write interactive articles, with the best user article getting featured next to the model on Hugging Face.
insights.speaker: Hannes von Essen
insights.so_what: Incentivizing user-generated interactive articles with featured placement is a low-cost content-flywheel play typical of community-led dev tools.
insights.quote: the best user article gets featured next to the model - it can therefore be a great way to both improve your own understanding of a model and get your name out at the same time!

insights.claim: The team built hfviewer because they viewed model visualization as a missing piece of the Hugging Face ecosystem, and supports it via a #hfviewer Discord channel for feature requests and graph-issue reports.
insights.speaker: Hannes von Essen
insights.so_what: Founder-as-user origin plus Discord-driven feedback loop is the standard indie dev-tool GTM; adoption depth still unproven.
insights.quote: we built it because we ourselves found it a missing piece of the Hugging Face ecosystem!
